name: L40S Architecture Optimization
approach: Target L40S architecture: 142 SMs, 100KB shared memory per SM, 864 GB/s GDDR6X bandwidth, 183 TFLOPS FP16. Lower bandwidth than A100/H100 makes memory-bound kernels harder to optimize. Focus on compute-bound optimizations, register blocking, and minimizing memory traffic. Grid multiples of 142.
pitfalls: L40S is the KernelBench leaderboard standard GPU — results on other GPUs are not directly comparable
GDDR6X bandwidth (864 GB/s) is much lower than HBM — memory-bound kernels benefit less from optimization
Shared memory per SM is 100KB — significantly less than H100 (192KB), plan tiles accordingly
